[Foundation] Improve NSArray.ArrayFromHandle significantly. (#24808)

* Enable nullability.
* Introduce numerous variations of ArrayFromHandle to handle various scenarios  related to nullability:
   * NonNullArrayFromHandle: returns an empty array if the native array is null.
   * ArrayFromHandleDropNullElements: returns an array that will never have any null elements.ç
   * NonNullArrayFromHandleDropNullElements: both of the above.
* Update consumers to use the best variation.
* Add tests for modified APIs that didn't already have tests.

Theoretically these changes may end up changing behavior, if there are native
arrays with NSNull elements, in which case we'll now convert them to managed
arrays without null elements for some APIs, but this should be exceedingly rare.

Contributes towards https://github.com/dotnet/macios/issues/17285.

[dotnet] Add support for @(RuntimeEnvironmentVariable). (#24824)

Add support for the '@(RuntimeEnvironmentVariable)' item group, which is set when using 'dotnet run' and passing environment variables using '-e|--environment NAME=VALUE' to 'dotnet run'.

This required adding the 'RuntimeEnvironmentVariableSupport' capability to tell 'dotnet run' that we support this.

References:

* https://github.com/dotnet/android/pull/10770
* https://github.com/dotnet/sdk/pull/52664

fix: Remove incompatible github-pages gem

github-pages v232 pins Jekyll to 3.10, which is incompatible with
jekyll-theme-chirpy 7.4 (requires Jekyll 4.4+). We don't need github-pages
for GitHub Actions custom builds—Actions uses bundle exec jekyll build
which respects our Gemfile with Jekyll 4.4.

Removing github-pages resolves the bundler conflict.

commit.message:
deploy.sh: suppress chmod error on migration storage if owned by container user

The storage dir may be owned by the www container user (UID 1000)
from a previous run. The deploy user can't chmod files it doesn't own,
but the dir is already writable — suppress the error with 2>/dev/null || true.

commit.message:
feat: Rc<str> strings, TDZ, wrapper objects, Set ES2025, Symbol fixes (#446)

* fix: typeof proxy returns 'function' when target is callable

Per ECMAScript §10.5.12, typeof on a Proxy should return 'function'
when the proxy's target has a [[Call]] internal method. Previously,
handle_type_of and handle_test_type_of always treated Proxy as 'object'.

- Add callable field to JsProxy to track target callability
- Add proxy_new_callable() constructor for callable proxy targets
- Add JsProxy::is_callable() accessor
- Update handle_type_of to return 'function' for callable proxies
- Update handle_test_type_of flag 6/7 to handle callable proxies
- Update Proxy/Proxy.revocable constructors to accept Function targets

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat: wrapper objects, callable proxy typeof, arguments iterator, Set ES2025 methods, String/RegExp symbol delegation

- Implement ToObject wrapper objects for primitives (Boolean, Number, String, Symbol, BigInt)
- Fix typeof for callable Proxy (returns 'function' when target is callable)
- Fix arguments @@iterator to return proper iterator over argument values
- Add String.prototype.matchAll() and String.prototype[@@iterator]()
- Add Array.prototype.toSpliced() and copyWithin()
- Add ES2025 Set methods: union, intersection, difference, symmetricDifference, isSubsetOf, isSupersetOf, isDisjointFrom
- Add Map.groupBy() static method
- String.prototype.match/replace/search/split/matchAll/replaceAll delegate to RegExp @@symbol methods

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Reject value changes on non-writable non-configurable properties

Add the ECMAScript §10.1.6.3 step 9.a.i check to define_own_property:
when a property is both non-configurable and non-writable, attempts to
change the value via defineProperty are rejected with TypeError (unless
the new value is SameValue to the existing one).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat: make Error properties settable via RefCell<PropertyMap> overlay

Add a props: RefCell<PropertyMap> overlay field to JsError so that
JS code can assign to error properties (e.g. rr.message = 'new',
rr.name = 'Custom', or arbitrary custom properties like rr.code).

- proto_lookup checks the overlay first before falling back to built-in
  fields (name, message, stack, cause, errors, toString, valueOf).
- StaNamedProperty and keyed_store now write to the overlay for Error.
- to_error_string() respects overridden name/message from the overlay
  (per ECMAScript §20.5.3.4).
- Added explicit 'constructor' key returning Undefined in proto_lookup.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: correct Symbol well-known symbol handling

- Fix Symbol.prototype[@@toStringTag] key: was using 'Symbol(4)' format
  instead of the '@@toStringTag' convention used throughout the codebase
- Add @@asyncIterator check in GetAsyncIterator (dispatch.rs): per
  §27.1.4.2 check @@asyncIterator first, fall back to @@iterator
- Add @@isConcatSpreadable support in Array.prototype.concat: respect
  the well-known symbol to determine whether objects should be spread

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Add JsValue::TheHole sentinel for Temporal Dead Zone checks

Previously the hole-checking opcodes (ThrowReferenceErrorIfHole,
ThrowSuperNotCalledIfHole, ThrowSuperAlreadyCalledIfNotHole) compared
against JsValue::Undefined. This is incorrect per the ECMAScript spec:
let x = undefined; x must succeed because the binding has been
initialised, even though its value is undefined.

Changes:
- Add JsValue::TheHole variant, an internal sentinel distinct from
  JsValue::Undefined, mirroring V8's the hole value.
- Add Opcode::LdaTheHole to load the sentinel into the accumulator.
- Update all three dispatch handlers to compare against TheHole.
- Add is_the_hole() predicate on JsValue.
- Handle TheHole in all exhaustive match sites (typeof, JSON, FFI,
  snapshot, heap snapshot, ToObject).
- Update interpreter tests to use LdaTheHole; add a new test proving
  that undefined no longer triggers a false TDZ error.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* perf: migrate JsValue::String(String) to JsValue::String(Rc<str>)

This changes the String variant of JsValue from owning a heap-allocated
String to a reference-counted Rc<str>. This makes JsValue::clone() O(1)
for string values (pointer copy + refcount bump) instead of O(n) (full
heap copy), which is the highest-impact performance improvement for the
interpreter since strings are the most frequently cloned value type.

Key changes across 22 files:
- JsValue::String variant now holds Rc<str> instead of String
- Constructor calls use .into() to convert String -> Rc<str>
- Pattern matches still work via Deref<Target=str>
- Functions returning String use .to_string() on the inner Rc<str>
- String method implementations (split, replace, etc.) convert to
  local String when mutable operations are needed
- Comparisons use &**s for Rc<str> == &str contexts

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
refactor: reorganize repository file structure (#133)

## Summary

Full repository reorganization for clarity and maintainability.

### src/ → grouped by concern
- **`src/server/`** — HTTP/WS server core (server, routes, auth,
websocket, sessions, preview)
- **`src/cli/`** — CLI tools & subcommands (arg parser, client, resume,
service, interactive, prompts)
- **`src/tunnel/`** — DevTunnel integration (tunnel, installer)
- **`src/utils/`** — Shared utilities (logger, shells, git, version,
update-check)
- **`src/frontend/`** — React SPA source (moved from root `frontend/`)

### test/ → mirrors src/
Tests reorganized into `test/server/`, `test/cli/`, `test/tunnel/`,
`test/utils/` subdirectories. Integration and E2E tests remain at
`test/` root.

### Root cleanup
- **`public-react/` → `public/`** — renamed, old vanilla JS `public/`
removed
- **`public/` gitignored** — it's a build artifact from `src/frontend/`,
rebuilt by `prepublishOnly`
- **`landing/` → `packages/landing/`** — independent sub-project
- **`demo-video/` → `packages/demo-video/`** — independent sub-project
- **`pmf/`** — empty directory removed

### Frontend components
- Split oversized `common/` into focused `Modals/`, `LoginPage/`, and
merged `CopyOverlay` into `Overlays/`

### Config updates
- All CI workflows, Dockerfile, package.json scripts, eslint, coverage
excludes, vite config updated
- npm `files` field uses explicit `src/` subdirectories to exclude
`src/frontend/` from package
- Dockerfile now builds frontend from source instead of copying
pre-built assets
- Copilot instructions updated to reflect new structure

### Verification
- ✅ 575/575 tests pass
- ✅ Lint passes
- ✅ Frontend builds correctly to `public/`
- ✅ `npm pack --dry-run` shows 32 files (no frontend source leak)
- ✅ No stale path references

---------

commit.message:
fix(openclaw-discord): upgrade to v2026.3.8 + config persistence fix (#143)

* fix(openclaw-discord): skip config generation if openclaw.json already exists

Preserve UI edits (bot accounts, policies) across Dokploy redeploys.
Previously, every redeploy would overwrite openclaw.json from env vars,
wiping any changes made via the OpenClaw dashboard (e.g., added Discord
accounts, allowlist configs).

Implement SysV AMD64 two-register convention for non-variadic 9–16 byte struct arguments (#869)

Co-authored-by: GregorGullwi <1397711+GregorGullwi@users.noreply.github.com>

* Implement SysV AMD64 two-register convention for non-variadic 9–16 byte struct args

Co-authored-by: GregorGullwi <1397711+GregorGullwi@users.noreply.github.com>

* The else-if branch and its body were under-indented relative to thematching if-branch. Align the } else if to 3 tabs (matching the if atline 1044), and indent the body contents by one additional tab each.

* Use isTwoRegisterStructRaw in typed-payload callee path

* Document known issue: two-register struct stack overflow only pushes one 8-byte slot

* Remove test_external_abi from expected link fail; fix two-reg struct stack overflow and space pre-alloc

Co-authored-by: GregorGullwi <1397711+GregorGullwi@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix handleFunctionCall second-pass register counter desync for two-register struct stack overflow

Co-authored-by: GregorGullwi <1397711+GregorGullwi@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix stale comment and indentation per Devin review feedback

Co-authored-by: GregorGullwi <1397711+GregorGullwi@users.noreply.github.com>

* Stack space calculation inconsistent with codegen for variadic two-register structs

Co-authored-by: devin-ai-integration[bot] <158243242+devin-ai-integration[bot]@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix two-register struct register check consistency (typed-payload path callee)

* Extract two-register struct helpers to eliminate duplication

Add three small methods shared by handleFunctionCall and
handleConstructorCall:
- resolveTypedValueFrameOffset: resolve StringHandle/TempVar to offset
- emitTwoRegStructToStack: store both halves to consecutive RSP slots
- emitTwoRegStructToRegs: load both halves into consecutive registers

This also fixes two constructor bugs:
1. First-pass stack overflow was missing two-register struct handling
2. Second-pass TempVar path was only loading the first 8 bytes

* Add test for constructor float stack overflow bug (missing stack_arg_count++ for float args). This test exercises the bug where handleConstructorCall's first passdoes not increment stack_arg_count after placing a float argument onthe stack.  When a subsequent integer argument also goes on the stack,it overwrites the float at the same RSP offset, corrupting the value.

* Fix missing stack_arg_count++ for float stack args in handleConstructorCall first pass

Without this increment, a subsequent stack argument (int or struct) is
written to the same RSP offset as the float, corrupting it. This mirrors
the correct pattern in handleFunctionCall at lines 237-238.

* Set shadow_stack_space for constructor calls too (Windows x64)

* Fix callee stack-param offset collision when float and int both overflow to stack

Co-authored-by: GregorGullwi <1397711+GregorGullwi@users.noreply.github.com>

* Add test for constructor double& parameter register mismatch bug

* Fix constructor second-pass is_float_arg to check !arg_is_reference, matching first pass

* Add formatting note about hard tabs to REVIEW.md

* Fix callee homing of double& params: add !param.is_reference to is_float_param check

Co-authored-by: GregorGullwi <1397711+GregorGullwi@users.noreply.github.com>

* New test that's expected to fail and fix indentation in test files

* Fix handleConstructorCall second pass: advance int_reg_index for stack-overflow args

When a non-float argument (especially a two-register struct) overflows the
register file in the constructor call second pass, int_reg_index was never
advanced. The first pass always advances temp_int_idx += regs_needed, so
subsequent arguments could be incorrectly classified as fitting in registers
when they were already placed on the stack. This mirrors the fix already
present in handleFunctionCall's second pass (lines 293-301).

* Add test for first-pass/second-pass param_shift desync in handleFunctionCall

This test exposes a pre-existing bug where handleFunctionCall's first pass
starts temp_int_idx=0 but the second pass starts int_reg_index=param_shift.
When param_shift=1 (function returns >16-byte struct by value via hidden
return parameter), the 6th integer argument is never placed on the stack
(first pass thinks it fits in registers) and never loaded into a register
(second pass sees it as overflowing), resulting in garbage for that parameter.

* Fix computeSysVOutgoingBytes to account for hidden return param

The stack space pre-scan now uses call_op->uses_return_slot to pass the
correct int_slots_start (0 or 1) to computeSysVOutgoingBytes, matching
the codegen's temp_int_idx = param_shift initialization.

* Fix constructor ABI handling for stack-passed structs

* Fix stale CallOp return-slot access

* Fix -Wshadow error: rename shadowed object_offset in handleConstructorCall

commit.message:
feat: initialize OpenInsure platform — AI-native core insurance system

Complete project foundation for an AI-native open-source core insurance platform
built on Microsoft Foundry + Azure, targeting Cyber Insurance MVP (Phase 1).

Core Domain Model:
- Party, Submission, Policy, Claim, Product, Billing entities (Pydantic v2)
- 13 domain events for event-driven architecture
- ACORD-aligned data model with modern JSON/REST APIs

REST API (FastAPI):
- Full CRUD for submissions, policies, claims, products, billing
- Compliance endpoints (decision records, audit trail, bias reports)
- Health checks and readiness probes (47 routes total)

AI Agent Framework:
- Base agent with EU AI Act DecisionRecord logging
- 8 specialized agents: submission, underwriting, policy, claims,
  document, knowledge, compliance, and multi-agent orchestrator
- New business workflow (7 steps) and claims workflow (3 steps)
- Authority management with configurable escalation thresholds

Azure Infrastructure (Bicep IaC):
- Azure SQL (Entra-only auth), Cosmos DB Gremlin (knowledge graph)
- AI Search (hybrid vector+keyword), Blob Storage, Event Grid, Service Bus
- Managed Identity with least-privilege RBAC (6 role assignments)
- Azure Monitor + Application Insights

Compliance Layer:
- Decision Record store (EU AI Act Art. 12 Record-Keeping)
- Immutable audit trail for all state changes
- Bias monitoring with 4/5ths disparate impact detection

Knowledge Base:
- Cyber liability SMB product definition (5 coverages, 5 rating factors)
- Underwriting guidelines (risk appetite, referral triggers, authority levels)
- US regulatory requirements with EU AI Act applicability notes

Additional:
- MCP Server interface (6 tools, 4 resource types)
- Cyber insurance rating engine with factor-based pricing
- Policy lifecycle (bind/endorse/renew/cancel) and claims processing services
- 177 passing tests, ruff lint clean, formatted
- GitHub Actions CI pipeline (lint, type-check, security scan, tests)
- AGENTS.md with full development team definition
- CONTRIBUTING.md, SECURITY.md, CHANGELOG.md, ADRs
